title: Use Display Name Instead of ID

story.so_that: see human-readable names instead of IDs for promotion types
story.i_want: the system to display display names instead of internal IDs

journey.before: User navigates to the tactics management interface, looking for tactics associated with specific promotion types
journey.during: User searches or filters tactics by promotion type, expecting to see human-readable names in the tactic view, search results table, and filter dropdown
journey.after: User can quickly identify and work with tactics by promotion type without needing to reference ID-to-name mappings

capability_breakdown.core_action: Display human-readable promotion type names instead of internal IDs across the UI (tactic view, search results, filter dropdown)
outputs: Human-readable promotion type name displayed in tactic view
Human-readable promotion type name displayed in search results table
Human-readable promotion type name displayed in filter dropdown
state_changes: UI rendering changes to show display_name field instead of ID

api: No API changes required (display_name already available from backend)
database: No database changes required (display_name field already exists)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Promotion type displayed as human-readable name in tactic view
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am a registered Internal Requester When I view a Tactic Then the promotion type is displayed as a human-readable name AND the display name is consistent across all views
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: View promotion type as human-readable name

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Search results table displays promotion types as human-readable names
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester When I search for tactics Then the search table displays promotion types as human-readable names
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: See promotion type names in search results

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Filter dropdown displays promotion types as human-readable names
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester When I open the select filter for promotion type Then the filter displays promotion types as human-readable names AND the Search filter works as before
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Filter tactics by human-readable promotion type names

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Display names are consistent across all views
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am an Internal Requester When I view the same promotion type in tactic view, search results, and filter dropdown Then the display name is identical in all locations

edge_cases.criterion: Promotion type with null or missing display name
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a promotion type has no display name When the UI renders the tactic view, search results, or filter Then a fallback value is displayed (e.g., ID or 'Unknown')

edge_cases.criterion: Promotion type with very long display name
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a promotion type has a very long display name (100+ characters) When the UI renders the name in search results or filter Then the name is truncated or wrapped appropriately
edge_cases.jtbd: Handle long display names in constrained UI spaces

edge_cases.criterion: Promotion type with special characters in display name
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a promotion type has special characters in its display name When the UI renders the name Then special characters are properly escaped and displayed
edge_cases.jtbd: Handle special characters in display names

edge_cases.gherkin: Given no promotion types exist When the filter dropdown is opened Then an appropriate message is displayed (e.g., 'No promotion types available')

existing_code: Search results table component (display promotion type)
API service that fetches tactics and promotion types

related: Any story related to promotion type management or display

domain_knowledge: Promotion type concept and its role in the tactics system
User workflow for viewing, searching, and filtering tactics
technical_knowledge: Frontend component architecture and state management
Data binding and rendering patterns used in the application
stakeholder_questions: Are there other places in the UI where promotion type IDs are displayed that should also be updated?

open_questions.question: Is the display_name field already populated in the backend for all existing promotion types?
Should the filter maintain the ability to filter by ID as a fallback if display name is unavailable?
What is the maximum length for a promotion type display name?
Are there any performance implications when rendering display names across multiple components?

recommendation: This is a low-complexity, low-risk UI update story. The backend already provides the display_name field, so implementation is primarily a frontend concern. Recommend prioritizing this story as it improves user experience with minimal technical risk. Key implementation focus: ensure consistent display across all three UI locations (tactic view, search results, filter dropdown) and handle edge cases like missing or very long display names.
